reasoning: The document exhibits catastrophic content loss, with over 80% of the source material (51 sections) omitted or replaced by placeholders, as confirmed by both the LLM fidelity findings and the low shingling recall. Furthermore, structural integrity is severely compromised by the fabrication of tables and a massive discrepancy in heading counts between the JSON data and the HTML output.

concerns.analysis: The output document is missing the vast majority of the source material. A literal placeholder in the JSON sections array confirms that 51 sections (representing over 80% of the 60-page document) have been omitted. Missing content includes 'Think Accessibility' (p. 5), 'Access by Disability Type' (p. 6), 'Keyboard Access' (p. 7), and dozens of slides between pages 21 and 58.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains 60 pages of content including sections on 'Keyboard Access', 'Screen Reader Super Powers', 'MS Word Checklist', etc.

concerns.analysis: On page 8, the source PDF displays a complex set of data tables (Schedules D, E, F, G, H, and I) containing over 20 rows of data. The output identifies a table element but only provides a single row containing a truncated title fragment, failing to extract any of the actual data rows from the form image.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 8 form image containing tables for Schedules D through I with multiple data rows.
concerns.output_quote: A single table row with content: "Magnification – A Di"

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: On page 18 ('Links Practice'), the source document lists four 'Before' examples of bad link text (e.g., '1) Click here for more information...') and four 'After' examples. The output entirely omits the instructional 'Before' text, which constitutes 50% of the textual information on that slide.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1) Click here for more information on related events; 2) Applications are due by August 12th, and link to apply can be found here; 3) Visit Artsy’s website at https://www.artsy.net/; 4) You can learn more about Sally’s work on her website
concerns.output_quote: [The sentences listed above are absent from the JSON element]

concerns.description: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
concerns.analysis: There is a significant discrepancy between the number of headings in the semantic.json (17) and the rendered HTML (75). This exceeds the allowable 10% or 3+ heading threshold, indicating that the renderer is adding a large number of headings not present in the structured data.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document contains numerous headings across its 60 pages, consistent with the metadata count of 74.
concerns.output_quote: Headings in semantic.json: 17; Headings in rendered HTML: 75

concerns.description: Fabricated tables: baseline has 0 tables but output has 2
Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The text extracted into the table on page 8 is truncated incorrectly. Instead of reflecting the slide content, it shows 'Magnification – A Di', which is a nonsensical fragment of the slide title 'Magnification – A Different View'. This wording change prevents a user from receiving accurate information.
concerns.location: Magnification – A Different View, page 8, table element

concerns.analysis: The source document contains several interactive links, particularly on the 'Resources' page (p. 44) and the 'Get in touch' page (p. 60). The JSON only records 2 links, while the rendered HTML shows 20. This indicates a failure to properly extract and structure all valid links from the source into the semantic JSON, with many links being treated as plain text (e.g., in Section 61).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 44 contains 5 distinct resource URLs. Page 60 contains a website and an email address link.

action_items: Identify why the pipeline failed to process 51 sections and instead left placeholders.
Re-run the document through the conversion pipeline with updated extraction parameters.
Verify the table extraction logic to ensure data rows are not being dropped during visual analysis.
